---
title: "Orga_DMSO"
author: "Edgar Dusacre"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---
```{r upload package}
library(ggplot2) 
library(forcats)
library(tidyr)
library(questionr)
library(deSolve)
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(skimr)
library(gmodels)
library(ggrepel)
library(lazyeval)
library(ggpubr)
library(multcompView)
library(readr)
library(scales)
library(knitr)
library(wesanderson)
library(dplyr) 
library(lubridate)
library(report)
library(ecotox)
library(stats)
library(dunn.test)
library(boot)
library(gridExtra)
library(multcompView)
library(rcompanion)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(car)
library(emmeans)
library(PMCMRplus)
library(multcomp)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggpattern)
```

```{r palette colors}
Plasfito_C <- c("#80CAD0", "#36A7A7", "#276573", "#054959","#E3CCC0", "#F7EAE1", "#A48A7B", "#C6AF8D", "#49BED3", "#256CB4", "#008163", "#44B494", "#7BC6B9")

Plasfito_color <- c("GreenP4" = "#80CAD0", "GreenP3" = "#36A7A7", "GreenP2" = "#276573", "GreenP1" = "#054959","Brown1" = "#E3CCC0", "Brown2" = "#F7EAE1", "Brown3" = "#A48A7B", "Brown4" = "#C6AF8D", "Turquoise1" = "#49BED3", "Blue1" = "#256CB4", "Green1" = "#008163", "Green2" = "#44B494", "Green3" = "#7BC6B9")

Color_FN <- c("pa_\u03B333"= "#054959", 
              "pa_\u03B360"= "#36A7A7", 
              "pbs_\u03B435"= "#49BED3", 
              "pbs_\u03B460"= "#256CB4")

Color_FN2 <- c("pa_g33_aa"= "#054959", 
              "pa_g60_aa"= "#36A7A7", 
              "pbs_d60_aa"= "aquamarine", 
              "pbs_d60_aa"= "#256CB4")

Color_FN3 <- c("pa_\u03B333"= "#054959", 
              "pa_\u03B360"= "#0096FC", 
              "pbs_\u03B435"= "#9beaa1", 
              "pbs_\u03B460"= "#77655C")

Color_FN4 <- c("pa_\u03B333"= "#80CAD0", 
              "pa_\u03B360"= "#0039D8", 
              "pbs_\u03B435"= "#0a7011", 
              "pbs_\u03B460"= "#C6AF8D")

color <- c("PBS 0.35 r1" = "#33aa3c", 
                               "PBS 0.35 r2" = "#0a7011",
                               "PBS 0.60 r1" = "#C6AF8D", 
                               "PBS 0.60 r2" = "#A48A7B",
                               "PA 6 0.33 r1" = "#80CAD0", 
                               "PA 6 0.33 r2" = "#054959",
                               "PA 6 0.60 r1" = "#0096FC",
                               "PA 6 0.60 r2" = "#0039D8",
                               "PBS 0.35 pHa" = "#9beaa1")

replicats <- c("1"= "#054959", 
              "2"= "#36A7A7", 
              "3"= "aquamarine", 
              "4"= "#256CB4")

time <- c("0"= "aquamarine", 
              "25"= "#054959")

time2 <- c("0"= "aquamarine", 
              "6"= "#33aa3c",
              "60" = "#36A7A7",
              "12"= "#256CB4", 
              "25"= "#054959")

Color_real_FN <- c("dmso" = "#A48A7B", "ctl H2O" = "#256CB4", "BLANK" = "#256CB4", "blc" = "#A48A7B", "pa_a35" = "#4fd355", "pa_b33" = "#ca98d3", "pa_b95" = "#6e217c", "pa_g33" = "#92b78f", "pa_g60" = "#bbb3bc", "pbs_d35" = "#d3ad4f","pbs_d60" = "#c68640", "hppe_e425" = "#36A7A7")

Color_real_FN3 <- c("dmso" = "#A48A7B", "ctl H2O" = "#256CB4", "BLANK" = "#256CB4", "pa_a35" = "#4fd355", "pa_b33" = "#ca98d3", "pa_b95" = "#6e217c", "pa_g33" = "#92b78f", "PA6" = "#4fd355", "pbs_d35" = "#d3ad4f","PBS-PBAT" = "#c68640", "PE-PP" = "#36A7A7")

Color_real_FN2 <- c("dmso" = "#A48A7B", "ctl H2O" = "#256CB4", "BLANK" = "#256CB4", "pa_a33_aa" = "#4fd355", "pa_b33_aa" = "#ca98d3", "pa_b95_aa" = "#6e217c", "pa_g33_aa" = "#92b78f", "pa_g60_aa" = "#bbb3bc", "pbs_d60_aa" = "#d3ad4f","pbs_d60_aa" = "#c68640", "hppe_e4.25_aa" = "#36A7A7")

Color_code_FN <- c("dmso" = "#A48A7B", "ctl H2O" = "#256CB4", "BLANK" = "#256CB4", "pa_\u03B135" = "#4fd355", "pa_\u03B233" = "#ca98d3", "pa_\u03B295" = "#6e217c", "pa_\u03B333" = "#92b78f", "pa_\u03B360" = "#bbb3bc", "pbs_\u03B435" = "#d3ad4f","pbs_\u03B460" = "#c68640", "pe_\u03B54.25" = "#36A7A7")

Color_real_FN5 <- c("Blank" = "#A48A7B", "blank" = "#A48A7B", "SW" = "#256CB4", "Seawater control" = "#256CB4", "PA α35" = "#4fd355", "PA β33" = "#ca98d3", "PA β95" = "#6e217c", "PA γ33" = "#92b78f", "PA γ60" = "#bbb3bc", "PBS δ35" = "#d3ad4f","PBS δ60" = "#c68640", "PE-PP ε4.25" = "#36A7A7")


```
5 categories of compounds are large categories in "Bilan_Analyses_EPOC_Edgar_Dusacre_CEDRE.xlsx" and tehre 6 in "Bilan_Analyses_EPOC_Edgar_Dusacre.xlsx":
```{r import data}
library(readxl)
data0 <- read_excel("Bilan_Analyses_EPOC_Edgar_Dusacre.xlsx", 
    sheet = "R") 
data0

#46 - 6 = 40 --> 40 compounds quantified

CrossTable(data0$compounds)

data_bis <- read_excel("Bilan_Analyses_EPOC_Edgar_Dusacre_CEDRE.xlsx", 
    sheet = "Bilan_R") 
data_bis

data1 <- left_join(data0, data_bis, by = c("LD_(ng_mL)", "LQ_(ng_mL)", "compounds")) %>% 
  filter(!(compounds %in% c(
    "1-methylnaphtalene",
    "2-methylfluoranthene",
    "2-methylnaphtalene",
    "perylene",
    "benzoapyrene",
    "4-NP")))
data1

data_metals <- read_excel("df_PhD_def.xlsx") %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(state == "used", "EOL", state)) %>% 
  group_by(FN, state, rep) %>% 
  summarise(values_corr = sum(Conc_corr, na.rm = TRUE),
            values = sum(Concentration, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  mutate(compounds = "Metals", LD = NA, LQ = NA, avg_blc = NA, sd_blc = NA) %>%  #to get the values of LD, LQ, avg_blc and sd_blc please go in the R script for metals
  dplyr::select(FN, state, rep, compounds, LD, LQ, values, avg_blc, sd_blc, values_corr) %>% 
  mutate(FN = case_when(
    FN == "pa_a35" ~ "PA α35",
    FN == "pa_b33" ~ "PA β33", 
    FN == "pa_b95" ~ "PA β95",
    FN == "pa_g33" ~ "PA γ33",
    FN == "pa_g60" ~ "PA γ60",
    FN == "pbd_d35" ~ "PBS δ35",
    FN == "pbd_d60" ~ "PBS δ60",
    FN == "hppe_e425" ~ "PE-PP ε4.25",
    TRUE ~ FN  # Garde les autres valeurs inchangées
  ))
data_metals

CrossTable(data_metals$FN)

data_metals_cp <- read_excel("df_PhD_def.xlsx") %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(state == "used", "EOL", state)) %>% 
  rename(compounds = Metals, values =Concentration, avg_blc = Avg_blc, values_corr = Conc_corr) %>%
  mutate(LD = NA, LQ = NA, avg_blc = NA, sd_blc = NA) %>%  #to get the values of LD, LQ, avg_blc and sd_blc please go in the R script for metals
  dplyr::select(FN, state, rep, compounds, LD, LQ, values, avg_blc, sd_blc, values_corr) %>% 
  mutate(FN = case_when(
    FN == "pa_a35" ~ "PA α35",
    FN == "pa_b33" ~ "PA β33", 
    FN == "pa_b95" ~ "PA β95",
    FN == "pa_g33" ~ "PA γ33",
    FN == "pa_g60" ~ "PA γ60",
    FN == "pbd_d35" ~ "PBS δ35",
    FN == "pbd_d60" ~ "PBS δ60",
    FN == "hppe_e425" ~ "PE-PP ε4.25",
    TRUE ~ FN  # Garde les autres valeurs inchangées
  ))
data_metals_cp

data_metals_rep <- data_metals %>% 
  filter(rep == "3" | rep == "4" | rep == "5") %>% 
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>% 
  summarise(values_corr = mean(values_corr, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  mutate(rep = "3")
data_metals_rep

data_metal_short <- data_metals %>% 
  filter(rep == "1" | rep == "2") %>% 
  bind_rows(data_metals_rep) %>% 
  #change name FNs
  mutate(FN = case_when(
    FN == "pa_a35" ~ "PA α35",
    FN == "pa_b33" ~ "PA β33", 
    FN == "pa_b95" ~ "PA β95",
    FN == "pa_g33" ~ "PA γ33",
    FN == "pa_g60" ~ "PA γ60",
    FN == "pbd_d35" ~ "PBS δ35",
    FN == "pbd_d60" ~ "PBS δ60",
    FN == "hppe_e425" ~ "PE-PP ε4.25",
    TRUE ~ FN  # Garde les autres valeurs inchangées
  ))
data_metal_short

avg_metals_sum <- data_metal_short %>% 
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>% 
  summarise(n = n(),
            avg_corr = mean(values_corr, na.rm = T))
avg_metals_sum
```

When for a FN of a specific state the values of the 3 replicates are <LQ or <LD --> NA
When one or more values <LQ or <LD --> LQ/2 or LD/2


```{r tidydata}
compounds <- data1 %>% dplyr::select(compounds)

data2 <- data1 %>% 
  # mutate(across(
  #   .cols = where(is.character) & !c("compounds"), # Exclude metadata
  #   .fns = ~ case_when(
  #     .x == "<LQ" ~ as.character(`LQ_(ng_mL)`),
  #     .x == "<LD" ~ as.character(`LD_(ng_mL)`),
  #     TRUE ~ .x
  #   )
  # )) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-compounds) %>% 
  #mutate_if(is.character, as.double) %>%
  bind_cols(compounds) %>%
  pivot_longer(!c(`LD_(ng_mL)`, `LQ_(ng_mL)`, compounds), names_to = "FN", values_to = "values") %>% 
  #split the column FN in two columns with the first "_"
  separate(FN, into = c("rep", "FN"), sep = "_", extra = "merge") %>% 
  separate(FN, into = c("FN", "state"), sep = "_", extra = "merge") %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(state == "n", "new",
                         if_else(state == "u", "EOL", NA))) %>% 
  rename(LD = `LD_(ng_mL)`, LQ = `LQ_(ng_mL)`) %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, rep, compounds, LD, LQ, values) %>% 
  mutate(FN = case_when(
    FN %in% c("alpha", "PA α ø.35", "pa_a33", "α35") ~ "PA α35",
    FN %in% c("beta33", "PA β ø.33", "pa_b33", "β33") ~ "PA β33", 
    FN %in% c("beta95", "PA β ø.95", "pa_b95", "β95") ~ "PA β95",
    FN %in% c("gamma33", "PA γ ø.33", "pa_g33", "γ33") ~ "PA γ33",
    FN %in% c("gamma60", "PA γ ø.60", "pa_g60", "γ60") ~ "PA γ60",
    FN %in% c("delta35", "PBS δ ø.35", "pbs_d35", "δ35") ~ "PBS δ35",
    FN %in% c("delta60", "PBS δ ø.60", "pbs_d60", "δ60") ~ "PBS δ60",
    FN %in% c("epsi", "HPPE ε ø4.25", "pe_e4.25", "ε4,25") ~ "PE-PP ε4.25",
    TRUE ~ FN  # Garde les autres valeurs inchangées
  )) %>% 
  #LOD & LOQ transformations
  ##if for a same FN of same state and same compounds only <LD or/and <LQ values become NA
  # group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>%
  # mutate(
  #   # Is there at least one numeric value in this group?
  #   has_numeric = any(!values %in% c("<LD", "<LQ") & !is.na(values)),
  #   # If no numeric in the group and value is <LD or <LQ → NA
  #   values = if_else(
  #     !has_numeric & values %in% c("<LD", "<LQ"),
  #     NA_character_,
  #     values
  #   )
  # ) %>%
  # ungroup() %>%
  # dplyr::select(-has_numeric) %>% 
  ##if <LD or <lQ --> LQ/2 or LD/2
  mutate(values = if_else(values == "<LD", "-1", 
                          if_else(values == "<LQ", "-2", values))) %>% 
  mutate(values = as.numeric(values)) %>% 
  mutate(values = case_when(
    values == -2 ~ LQ/2,
    values == -1 ~ LD/2,
    TRUE ~ values
  ))
data2

data_no_LDLQ <- data2 %>% filter(LD != values & LQ != values)
data_no_LDLQ

Comp_cat <- data2 %>% filter(compounds %in% c("HAP", "PBDE", "Phtalates", "non_Phtalates", "AKP"))
Comp_cat

```

```{r avg}
data_avg <- data2 %>% 
  filter(!(compounds %in% c("HAP", "PBDE", "Phtalates", "non_Phtalates", "AKP", "AKP_NP"))) %>% 
   mutate(values = if_else(abs(values - LD) < 1e-6 | abs(values - LQ) < 1e-6, NA_real_, values)) %>% 
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>% 
  summarise( n = n(),
             avg = mean(values, na.rm = T),
             sd = sd(values, na.rm = T)) 
data_avg


data_sum_FN <- data_avg %>% 
  summarize(n_avg_gt0 = sum(avg > 0, na.rm = TRUE))
data_sum_FN

df <- data_avg %>%
  filter(avg > 0) 

data_avg_cat <- data2 %>% 
  filter((compounds %in% c("HAP", "PBDE", "Phtalates", "non_Phtalates", "AKP"))) %>% 
   #mutate(values = if_else(abs(values - LD) < 1e-6 | abs(values - LQ) < 1e-6, NA_real_, values)) %>% 
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>% 
  summarise( n = n(),
             avg = mean(values, na.rm = T),
             sd = sd(values, na.rm = T)) 
data_avg_cat

```


```{r corr blank substraction cat}
avg_blc_cat <- Comp_cat %>% 
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>% 
  summarise( n = n(),
             avg = mean(values, na.rm = T),
             sd = sd(values, na.rm = T)
             ) %>% 
  filter(FN == "blank") %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::select(-state, -n) %>%
  rename(avg_blc = avg, sd_blc = sd)
avg_blc_cat

data_corr_cat <- Comp_cat %>% 
  left_join(avg_blc_cat, by = c("compounds")) %>% 
  mutate(values_corr = values - avg_blc) %>% 
  mutate(values_corr = if_else(values_corr < 0, 0, values_corr)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-FN.y) %>% 
  rename(FN = FN.x) 
data_corr_cat

metal_orga <- data_corr_cat %>% 
  bind_rows(data_metals) %>% 
  mutate(compounds = case_when(
    compounds == "Phtalates" ~ "Phthalates",
    compounds == "non_Phtalates" ~ "Non phthalate plasticizers",
    TRUE ~ compounds
  ))
metal_orga

data_avg_corr_cat <- data_corr_cat %>% 
  filter(FN != "blank") %>% 
  group_by(state, compounds) %>% 
  summarise(n = n(),
             avg_corr = mean(values_corr, na.rm = T),
            sd_corr = sd(values_corr, na.rm = T)) %>% 
  mutate(avg_ng_gFN = avg_corr*0.003*1000,
         sd_ng_gFN = sd_corr*0.003*1000) #%>% 
#   filter(compounds == "HAP") %>% 
#   group_by(state, compounds) %>%
#   summarise(n = n(),
#              avg_corr = mean(avg_ng_gFN, na.rm = T),
#             sd_corr = sd(sd_ng_gFN, na.rm = T))
data_avg_corr_cat

#export excel
# library(writexl)
# write_xlsx(data_avg_corr_cat, "orga_dmso_avg_cat.xlsx")
```

```{r corr blank substraction compounds}
avg_blc <- data2 %>% 
  filter(!(compounds %in% c("HAP", "PBDE", "Phtalates", "non_Phtalates", "AKP", "AKP_NP"))) %>% 
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>% 
  summarise( n = n(),
             avg = mean(values, na.rm = T),
             sd = sd(values, na.rm = T)
             ) %>% 
  filter(FN == "blank") %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::select(-state, -n) %>%
  rename(avg_blc = avg, sd_blc = sd)
avg_blc

data_corr <- data2 %>% 
  filter(!(compounds %in% c("HAP", "PBDE", "Phtalates", "non_Phtalates", "AKP", "AKP_NP"))) %>% 
  left_join(avg_blc, by = c("compounds")) %>% 
  rename(FN = FN.x) %>% 
  mutate(values_corr = if_else(FN != "blank", values - avg_blc, values)) %>% 
  mutate(values_corr = if_else(values_corr < 0, 0, values_corr)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-FN.y)
data_corr

metal_orga_cp <- data_corr %>% 
  bind_rows(data_metals_cp) 
metal_orga_cp

data_avg_corr <- metal_orga_cp %>% 
  filter(!(compounds %in% c("HAP", "PBDE", "Phtalates", "non_Phtalates", "AKP", "AKP_NP"))) %>% 
  #filter(FN != "blank") %>% 
  filter(!(compounds %in% c(
    "1-methylnaphtalene",
    "2-methylfluoranthene",
    "2-methylnaphtalene",
    "perylene",
    "benzoapyrene",
    "4-NP"))) %>%
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>% 
  summarise(n = n(),
            avg = mean(values, na.rm = T),
             sd = sd(values, na.rm = T),
             avg_corr = mean(values_corr, na.rm = T),
             sd_corr = sd(values_corr, na.rm = T)) 
data_avg_corr

nb_compound_by_FN <- data_avg_corr %>% 
  mutate(cat = case_when(
    compounds %in% c("As", "Ag", "Al", "Cu", "Fe", "Zn", "Mn") ~ "metals",
    TRUE ~ "organic"
  )) %>% 
  filter(avg_corr >0 & sd_corr >0) %>% 
  #don't tale into account the trace metals
  #filter(cat != "metals") %>% 
  group_by(FN, state, cat) %>% 
  summarise(
    n_compounds = n_distinct(compounds),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(percentage = case_when(
    cat == "metals" ~ n_compounds *100 /7,
    cat == "organic" ~ n_compounds *100 /34))
nb_compound_by_FN


# #export excel
# library(writexl)
# write_xlsx(data_avg_corr, "orga_dmso_avg.xlsx")
```


Compounds with values below LD or LD:
1-methylnaphtalene
2-methylfluoranthene
2-methylnaphtalene
perylene
benzoapyrene

4-NP

Results of the nonylphenol seems wrong due to high strange values for the FNs:

##Statistics large categories
```{r Anova ou kruskal including blanks - new FNs}
# ANOVA or Kruskal-Wallis test including blanks
df_new <- metal_orga %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>%
  filter(state != "EOL") %>% 
  arrange(compounds) 
df_new

CrossTable(df_new$compounds)

res_aov <- df_new %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    aov_model = map(data, ~ aov(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    aov_tidy  = map(aov_model, ~ broom::tidy(.x)),
    resid     = map(aov_model, residuals),
    shapiro   = map(resid, ~ shapiro.test(.x)),
    bartlett  = map(data, ~ bartlett.test(values ~ FN, data = .x))
  )

res_aov$shapiro

res_kw <- df_new %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    kw_test  = map(data, ~ kruskal.test(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    kw_tidy  = map(kw_test, ~ broom::tidy(.x))
  )
res_kw$kw_tidy

# 2) Keep only compounds with p.value < 0.05
res_kw_sig <- res_kw %>% 
  mutate(p_value = map_dbl(kw_tidy, ~ .x$p.value)) %>% 
  filter(p_value < 0.05)
res_kw_sig

# 3) Dunn test for those significant compounds
res_dunn <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    dunn_raw = map(data, ~ FSA::dunnTest(values ~ FN, data = .x, method = "holm")),
    dunn_tbl = map(dunn_raw, ~ .x$res %>% as_tibble())
  )

# 4) Flat table of all post-hoc comparisons
dunn_table <- res_dunn %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, dunn_tbl) %>%   # use the real column name
  unnest(dunn_tbl)
dunn_table

# 3bis) Dunn test + letters for significant compounds only
res_letters <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    # Dunn all-pairs test per compound
    dunn_kw   = map(data, ~ kwAllPairsDunnTest(values ~ as.factor(FN),
                                               data = .x,
                                               p.adjust.method = "holm")),
    # matrix of adjusted p-values
    p_mat     = map(dunn_kw, ~ round(.x$p.value, digits = 3)),
    # full symmetric matrix required by multcompLetters
    p_full    = map(p_mat, fullPTable),
    # compact letter display
    letters   = map(p_full, ~ multcompLetters(.x)$Letters),
    # convert named vector of letters to tibble
    letters_df = map(letters, ~ tibble(
      FN = names(.x),
      label = as.vector(unlist(.x))
    ))
  )

letters_table_new <- res_letters %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, letters_df) %>% 
  unnest(letters_df)
letters_table_new
```

```{r Anova ou kruskal including blanks - EOL FNs}
# ANOVA or Kruskal-Wallis test including blanks
df_EOL <- metal_orga %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>%
  filter(state != "new") %>% 
  arrange(compounds)
df_EOL

res_aov <- df_EOL %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    aov_model = map(data, ~ aov(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    aov_tidy  = map(aov_model, ~ broom::tidy(.x)),
    resid     = map(aov_model, residuals),
    shapiro   = map(resid, ~ shapiro.test(.x)),
    bartlett  = map(data, ~ bartlett.test(values ~ FN, data = .x))
  )

res_aov$shapiro

res_kw <- df_EOL %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    kw_test  = map(data, ~ kruskal.test(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    kw_tidy  = map(kw_test, ~ broom::tidy(.x))
  )
res_kw$kw_tidy

# 2) Keep only compounds with p.value < 0.05
res_kw_sig <- res_kw %>% 
  mutate(p_value = map_dbl(kw_tidy, ~ .x$p.value)) %>% 
  filter(p_value < 0.05)
res_kw_sig

# 3) Dunn test for those significant compounds
res_dunn <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    dunn_raw = map(data, ~ FSA::dunnTest(values ~ FN, data = .x, method = "holm")),
    dunn_tbl = map(dunn_raw, ~ .x$res %>% as_tibble())
  )

# 4) Flat table of all post-hoc comparisons
dunn_table <- res_dunn %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, dunn_tbl) %>%   # use the real column name
  unnest(dunn_tbl)
dunn_table

# 3bis) Dunn test + letters for significant compounds only
res_letters <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    # Dunn all-pairs test per compound
    dunn_kw   = map(data, ~ kwAllPairsDunnTest(values ~ as.factor(FN),
                                               data = .x,
                                               p.adjust.method = "holm")),
    # matrix of adjusted p-values
    p_mat     = map(dunn_kw, ~ round(.x$p.value, digits = 3)),
    # full symmetric matrix required by multcompLetters
    p_full    = map(p_mat, fullPTable),
    # compact letter display
    letters   = map(p_full, ~ multcompLetters(.x)$Letters),
    # convert named vector of letters to tibble
    letters_df = map(letters, ~ tibble(
      FN = names(.x),
      label = as.vector(unlist(.x))
    ))
  )

letters_table_EOL <- res_letters %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, letters_df) %>% 
  unnest(letters_df)
letters_table_EOL
```

```{r df with all letters KW including blanks}
results_KW_blc <- bind_rows(
  letters_table_new %>% mutate(state = "new"),
  letters_table_EOL %>% mutate(state = "EOL")
) %>%
  #mutate(state = if_else(FN == "blank", "blank", state)) %>% 
  arrange(compounds, state, FN) %>% 
  #if state = new label in capital letters
  mutate(
    label = case_when(
      state == "new" ~ toupper(label),
      TRUE ~ label))
results_KW_blc 
```

```{r Anova ou kruskal excluding blanks - new FNs}
# ANOVA or Kruskal-Wallis test including blanks
df_new <- data_corr_cat %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>%
  filter(state != "EOL", FN != "blank") 
df_new

res_aov <- df_new %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    aov_model = map(data, ~ aov(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    aov_tidy  = map(aov_model, ~ broom::tidy(.x)),
    resid     = map(aov_model, residuals),
    shapiro   = map(resid, ~ shapiro.test(.x)),
    bartlett  = map(data, ~ bartlett.test(values ~ FN, data = .x))
  )

res_aov$shapiro

res_kw <- df_new %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    kw_test  = map(data, ~ kruskal.test(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    kw_tidy  = map(kw_test, ~ broom::tidy(.x))
  )
res_kw$kw_tidy

# 2) Keep only compounds with p.value < 0.05
res_kw_sig <- res_kw %>% 
  mutate(p_value = map_dbl(kw_tidy, ~ .x$p.value)) %>% 
  filter(p_value < 0.05)

# 3) Dunn test for those significant compounds
res_dunn <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    dunn_raw = map(data, ~ FSA::dunnTest(values ~ FN, data = .x, method = "holm")),
    dunn_tbl = map(dunn_raw, ~ .x$res %>% as_tibble())
  )

# 4) Flat table of all post-hoc comparisons
dunn_table <- res_dunn %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, dunn_tbl) %>%   # use the real column name
  unnest(dunn_tbl)
dunn_table

# 3bis) Dunn test + letters for significant compounds only
res_letters <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    # Dunn all-pairs test per compound
    dunn_kw   = map(data, ~ kwAllPairsDunnTest(values ~ as.factor(FN),
                                               data = .x,
                                               p.adjust.method = "holm")),
    # matrix of adjusted p-values
    p_mat     = map(dunn_kw, ~ round(.x$p.value, digits = 3)),
    # full symmetric matrix required by multcompLetters
    p_full    = map(p_mat, fullPTable),
    # compact letter display
    letters   = map(p_full, ~ multcompLetters(.x)$Letters),
    # convert named vector of letters to tibble
    letters_df = map(letters, ~ tibble(
      FN = names(.x),
      label = as.vector(unlist(.x))
    ))
  )

letters_table_new <- res_letters %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, letters_df) %>% 
  unnest(letters_df)
letters_table_new
```

```{r Anova ou kruskal excluding blanks - EOL FNs}
# ANOVA or Kruskal-Wallis test including blanks
df_EOL <- data_corr_cat %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>%
  filter(state != "new", FN != "blank") 
df_EOL

res_aov <- df_EOL %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    aov_model = map(data, ~ aov(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    aov_tidy  = map(aov_model, ~ broom::tidy(.x)),
    resid     = map(aov_model, residuals),
    shapiro   = map(resid, ~ shapiro.test(.x)),
    bartlett  = map(data, ~ bartlett.test(values ~ FN, data = .x))
  )

res_aov$shapiro

res_kw <- df_EOL %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    kw_test  = map(data, ~ kruskal.test(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    kw_tidy  = map(kw_test, ~ broom::tidy(.x))
  )
res_kw$kw_tidy

# 2) Keep only compounds with p.value < 0.05
res_kw_sig <- res_kw %>% 
  mutate(p_value = map_dbl(kw_tidy, ~ .x$p.value)) %>% 
  filter(p_value < 0.05)

# 3) Dunn test for those significant compounds
res_dunn <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    dunn_raw = map(data, ~ FSA::dunnTest(values ~ FN, data = .x, method = "holm")),
    dunn_tbl = map(dunn_raw, ~ .x$res %>% as_tibble())
  )

# 4) Flat table of all post-hoc comparisons
dunn_table <- res_dunn %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, dunn_tbl) %>%   # use the real column name
  unnest(dunn_tbl)
dunn_table

# 3bis) Dunn test + letters for significant compounds only
res_letters <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    # Dunn all-pairs test per compound
    dunn_kw   = map(data, ~ kwAllPairsDunnTest(values ~ as.factor(FN),
                                               data = .x,
                                               p.adjust.method = "holm")),
    # matrix of adjusted p-values
    p_mat     = map(dunn_kw, ~ round(.x$p.value, digits = 3)),
    # full symmetric matrix required by multcompLetters
    p_full    = map(p_mat, fullPTable),
    # compact letter display
    letters   = map(p_full, ~ multcompLetters(.x)$Letters),
    # convert named vector of letters to tibble
    letters_df = map(letters, ~ tibble(
      FN = names(.x),
      label = as.vector(unlist(.x))
    ))
  )

letters_table_EOL <- res_letters %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, letters_df) %>% 
  unnest(letters_df)
letters_table_EOL
```

```{r df with all letters KW excluding blanks}
results_KW_nblc <- bind_rows(
  letters_table_new %>% mutate(state = "new"),
  letters_table_EOL %>% mutate(state = "EOL")
) %>%
  #mutate(state = if_else(FN == "blank", "blank", state)) %>% 
  arrange(compounds, state, FN) %>% 
  #if state = new label in capital letters
  mutate(
    label = case_when(
      state == "new" ~ toupper(label),
      TRUE ~ label))
results_KW_nblc 
```


```{r Statistics new vs EOL}
df_new_EOL <- metal_orga %>% 
  filter(FN != "blank") 
df_new_EOL

#Eliminate the FN and state for which the values of the three replicates were = 0
df_counts <-
  df_new_EOL %>%
  group_by(FN, compounds, state) %>%
  summarise(
    n_zero = sum(!is.na(values_corr) & values_corr == 0),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

compounds_ok <-
  df_counts %>%
  group_by(FN, compounds) %>%
  filter(all(n_zero < 3)) %>%   # remove FN/compound with any state having 3 zeros
  ungroup() %>%
  distinct(FN, compounds)

df_new_EOL_ok <-
  df_new_EOL %>%
  semi_join(compounds_ok, by = c("FN", "compounds"))
df_new_EOL_ok

results_tests <-
  df_new_EOL_ok %>%
  group_by(compounds, FN) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    # simple one-way model: values_corr ~ state
    mod <- lm(values_corr ~ state, data = .x)
    
    # Shapiro–Wilk on residuals (normality)
    shapiro_p <- shapiro.test(residuals(mod))$p.value
    
    # Levene’s test (homogeneity of variance across states)
    lev_p <- leveneTest(values_corr ~ state, data = .x)$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    
    tibble(
      shapiro_p = shapiro_p,
      levene_p  = lev_p,
      n         = n_distinct(.x$state)
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup()
results_tests

# One MWU test per FN × compounds, comparing new vs EOL
tt_results <- df_new_EOL_ok %>% 
  group_by(FN, compounds) %>% 
  summarise(
    tt = list(t.test(values_corr ~ state, var.equal = T)),  # t test
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    tt_tidy = map(tt, broom::tidy)
  ) %>% 
  unnest(tt_tidy) %>% 
  mutate(
    diff = if_else(p.value < 0.0001, "****", 
                   if_else(p.value < 0.001, "***",
                           if_else(p.value < 0.01, "**",
                                   if_else(p.value < 0.05, "*", "ns"))))
  ) 
tt_results


```


```{r Statistics blank vs. FN and state}

#1) Prepare data

df <- metal_orga %>%
  filter(!is.na(values)) %>% 
  filter(!compounds %in% c("Metals"))


# blank replicated for each state so comparisons are "compound + state"
blank_states <- tibble(state = c("new", "EOL"))

df_blank <- df %>%
  filter(FN == "blank") %>%
  dplyr::select(FN, compounds, values) %>%
  crossing(blank_states)

df_test <- df %>%
  filter(FN != "blank", state %in% c("new", "EOL")) %>%
  dplyr::select(FN, state, compounds, values)

# 2) Run MWU test: FN vs blank
#    for each (FN, state, compounds)

results_vs_blank <- df_test %>%
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>%
  summarise(
    n_FN = sum(!is.na(values)),
    x = list(values),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    df_blank %>%
      group_by(state, compounds) %>%
      summarise(
        n_blank = sum(!is.na(values)),
        y = list(values),
        .groups = "drop"
      ),
    by = c("state", "compounds")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Welch t-test only if both groups have enough data + some variability
    p_value = pmap_dbl(
      list(x, y, n_FN, n_blank),
      ~{
        if (..3 < 2 || ..4 < 2) return(NA_real_)
        if (dplyr::n_distinct(..1) < 2 && dplyr::n_distinct(..2) < 2) return(NA_real_)
        suppressWarnings(wilcox.test(..1, ..2, paired = FALSE)$p.value)
      }
    ),
    stars = case_when(
      is.na(p_value)  ~ NA_character_,
      p_value < 0.001 ~ "###",
      p_value < 0.01  ~ "##",
      p_value < 0.05  ~ "#",
      TRUE            ~ "ns"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(FN, state, compounds, n_FN, n_blank, p_value, stars) %>%
  arrange(compounds, state, FN) #%>% 
  #filter(stars != "ns")

results_vs_blank


```

```{r test homogeneity of the variance}

df <- metal_orga %>%
  filter(!is.na(values)) %>% 
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>%
  filter(n() >= 3) %>%                    # keep only groups with ≥ 3 rows
  ungroup() #%>% 
  #filter(compounds %in% c("Metals"))

# 1) store blanks once (they have FN == "blank", state == NA)
df_blanks <- df %>%
  filter(FN == "blank") %>%
  dplyr::select(FN, state, rep, compounds, LD, LQ, values, avg_blc, sd_blc, values_corr)

# 2) for each FN-state-compound (non-blank), bind the corresponding blank rows (same compound)
lst_FN_state_compound <- df %>%
  filter(FN != "blank") %>%
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>%
  group_split(.keep = TRUE) %>%                         # list of tibbles (one per FN-state-compound) [web:8]
  map(\(.x) bind_rows(
    .x,
    df_blanks %>% filter(compounds == .x$compounds[1])
  ))

# optional: name the list for easier access
keys <- df %>%
  filter(FN != "blank") %>%
  distinct(FN, state, compounds) %>%
  mutate(id = paste(FN, state, compounds, sep = "_"))

names(lst_FN_state_compound) <- keys$id

# lst_FN_state_compound[[1]]
# lst_FN_state_compound[["PA α35_new_HAP"]]

bartlett_by_df <- imap_dfr(
  lst_FN_state_compound,
  \(d, id) {
    # Bartlett needs >=2 observations in each FN level (here: blank vs your FN)
    n_by_FN <- d %>% count(FN)

    ok <- (n_distinct(d$FN) == 2) && all(n_by_FN$n >= 2)  # blank + sample, each >=2 [web:49]

    out <- if (ok) {
      broom::tidy(bartlett.test(values ~ FN, data = d))   # returns statistic/parameter/p.value/method [web:22]
    } else {
      tibble(
        statistic = NA_real_,
        parameter = NA_real_,
        p.value   = NA_real_,
        method    = "Bartlett test of homogeneity of variances"
      )
    }

    tibble(id = id, ok = ok) %>%
      bind_cols(out)
      
  }
)

bartlett_by_df

df_bartlett <- bartlett_by_df %>% 
  tidyr::separate(
    col  = id,
    into = c("FN", "state", "compounds"),
    sep  = "_",
    remove = TRUE
  ) %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, compounds, p.value) %>% 
  mutate(res = case_when(
    p.value < 0.05 ~ "variances different",
    p.value >= 0.05 ~ "variances homogeneous",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))
df_bartlett 
```


students better for metals because normal distri + homogeneity of variance

```{r test statistics blc vs FN metals student}

#1) Prepare data

df <- metal_orga %>%
  filter(!is.na(values)) %>% 
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>%
  filter(n() >= 3) %>%                    # keep only groups with ≥ 3 rows
  ungroup() %>% 
  filter(compounds %in% c("Metals"))

# blank replicated for each state so comparisons are "compound + state"
blank_states <- tibble(state = c("new", "EOL"))

df_blank <- df %>%
  filter(FN == "blank") %>%
  dplyr::select(FN, compounds, values) %>%
  crossing(blank_states)

df_test <- df %>%
  filter(FN != "blank", state %in% c("new", "EOL")) %>%
  dplyr::select(FN, state, compounds, values)


# 1. Long data with group indicator
df_all <- bind_rows(
  df_test  %>% mutate(group = "FN"),
  df_blank %>% mutate(group = "blank")
)

# 2. Shapiro–Wilk normality per FN/state/compound and group
normality_results <- df_all %>%
  group_by(FN, state, compounds, group) %>%
  summarise(
    shapiro_p = if (sum(!is.na(values)) >= 3 && dplyr::n_distinct(values) > 1) {
      shapiro.test(values)$p.value
    } else {
      NA_real_
    },
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3. Levene test FN vs blank per state/compound
levene_results <- df_all %>%
  filter(!is.na(values)) %>%
  group_by(state, compounds) %>%
  summarise(
    levene_p = tryCatch(
      car::leveneTest(values, group)$`Pr(>F)`[1],
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 4. Combine and choose Student / Welch / Wilcoxon
results_vs_blank_auto <- df_test %>%
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>%
  summarise(
    n_FN = sum(!is.na(values)),
    x = list(values),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # keep an explicit copy of FN that will survive joins
  mutate(FN_source = FN) %>%
  left_join(
    df_blank %>%
      group_by(state, compounds) %>%
      summarise(
        n_blank = sum(!is.na(values)),
        y = list(values),
        .groups = "drop"
      ),
    by = c("state", "compounds")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    normality_results %>%
      filter(group == "FN") %>%
      rename(shapiro_p_FN = shapiro_p) %>%
      dplyr::select(-group),
    by = c("FN" = "FN", "state", "compounds")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    normality_results %>%
      filter(group == "blank") %>%
      rename(shapiro_p_blank = shapiro_p) %>%
      dplyr::select(-group),
    by = c("state", "compounds")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    levene_results,
    by = c("state", "compounds")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    test_type = case_when(
      is.na(shapiro_p_FN) | is.na(shapiro_p_blank) ~ "wilcox",
      shapiro_p_FN  > 0.03 &
        shapiro_p_blank > 0.03 &
        !is.na(levene_p) &
        levene_p > 0.05 ~ "student",
      shapiro_p_FN  > 0.03 &
        shapiro_p_blank > 0.05 &
        !is.na(levene_p) &
        levene_p <= 0.05 ~ "welch",
      TRUE ~ "wilcox"
    ),
    p_value = pmap_dbl(
      list(x, y, n_FN, n_blank, test_type),
      ~{
        if (..3 < 2 || ..4 < 2) return(NA_real_)
        if (dplyr::n_distinct(..1) < 2 && dplyr::n_distinct(..2) < 2) return(NA_real_)

        if (..5 == "student") {
          suppressWarnings(t.test(..1, ..2,
                                  var.equal = TRUE,
                                  paired = FALSE)$p.value)
        } else if (..5 == "welch") {
          suppressWarnings(t.test(..1, ..2,
                                  var.equal = FALSE,
                                  paired = FALSE)$p.value)
        } else {
          suppressWarnings(wilcox.test(..1, ..2,
                                       paired = FALSE)$p.value)
        }
      }
    ),
    stars = case_when(
      is.na(p_value)  ~ NA_character_,
      p_value < 0.001 ~ "###",
      p_value < 0.01  ~ "##",
      p_value < 0.05  ~ "#",
      TRUE            ~ "ns"
    )
  ) %>%
  # recreate FN from the preserved copy, then select
  mutate(FN = FN_source) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    FN, state, compounds,
    n_FN, n_blank,
    shapiro_p_FN, shapiro_p_blank, levene_p, test_type,
    p_value, stars
  ) %>%
  arrange(compounds, state, FN, test_type) #%>% 
  #replace results of wilcox test by ns because not meaningfull and useless to show it because very low power
  # mutate(stars_corr   = if_else(test_type == "wilcox", "ns", stars)
  # ) %>% 
  # filter(stars_corr != "ns") %>% 
  # dplyr::select(FN,          state, compounds,  n_FN, n_blank, p_value, stars)


results_vs_blank_auto

#compare tables
compare <- results_vs_blank_auto %>%
  left_join(results_vs_blank %>% mutate(test_type = "wilcox"),
            by = c("FN", "state", "compounds")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, compounds, test_type.x, test_type.y, stars.x, stars.y) %>% 
  mutate(compare = if_else(grepl("#", stars.x) & grepl("#", stars.y) | stars.x == stars.y, "ok", "diff"))
compare

results_vs_blank_metals <- results_vs_blank_auto %>% 
  dplyr::select(FN, state, compounds, n_FN, n_blank, p_value, stars)

results_blc_FN <- results_vs_blank %>% 
  bind_rows(results_vs_blank_metals)
results_blc_FN
```


##Statistics individual compounds
```{r Anova ou kruskal including blanks - new FNs}
# ANOVA or Kruskal-Wallis test including blanks
df_new_cp <- metal_orga_cp %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>%
  filter(state != "EOL") 
df_new_cp

# res_aov <- df_new_cp %>% 
#   group_by(compounds) %>% 
#   nest() %>% 
#   mutate(
#     aov_model = map(data, ~ aov(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
#     aov_tidy  = map(aov_model, ~ broom::tidy(.x)),
#     resid     = map(aov_model, residuals),
#     shapiro   = map(resid, ~ shapiro.test(.x)),
#     bartlett  = map(data, ~ bartlett.test(values ~ FN, data = .x))
#   )
# 
# res_aov$shapiro

res_kw <- df_new_cp %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    kw_test  = map(data, ~ kruskal.test(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    kw_tidy  = map(kw_test, ~ broom::tidy(.x))
  )
res_kw$kw_tidy

# 2) Keep only compounds with p.value < 0.05
res_kw_sig <- res_kw %>% 
  mutate(p_value = map_dbl(kw_tidy, ~ .x$p.value)) %>% 
  filter(p_value < 0.05)

# 3) Dunn test for those significant compounds
res_dunn <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    dunn_raw = map(data, ~ FSA::dunnTest(values ~ FN, data = .x, method = "holm")),
    dunn_tbl = map(dunn_raw, ~ .x$res %>% as_tibble())
  )

# 4) Flat table of all post-hoc comparisons
dunn_table <- res_dunn %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, dunn_tbl) %>%   # use the real column name
  unnest(dunn_tbl)
dunn_table

# 3bis) Dunn test + letters for significant compounds only
res_letters <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    # Dunn all-pairs test per compound
    dunn_kw   = map(data, ~ kwAllPairsDunnTest(values ~ as.factor(FN),
                                               data = .x,
                                               p.adjust.method = "holm")),
    # matrix of adjusted p-values
    p_mat     = map(dunn_kw, ~ round(.x$p.value, digits = 3)),
    # full symmetric matrix required by multcompLetters
    p_full    = map(p_mat, fullPTable),
    # compact letter display
    letters   = map(p_full, ~ multcompLetters(.x)$Letters),
    # convert named vector of letters to tibble
    letters_df = map(letters, ~ tibble(
      FN = names(.x),
      label = as.vector(unlist(.x))
    ))
  )

letters_table_new_cp <- res_letters %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, letters_df) %>% 
  unnest(letters_df) %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(label) > 1) %>%   # keep compounds having ≥2 different labels
  ungroup()
  
letters_table_new_cp
```

```{r Anova ou kruskal including blanks - EOL FNs}
# ANOVA or Kruskal-Wallis test including blanks
df_EOL_cp <- metal_orga_cp %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>%
  filter(state != "new") 
df_EOL_cp

# res_aov <- df_EOL_cp %>% 
#   group_by(compounds) %>% 
#   nest() %>% 
#   mutate(
#     aov_model = map(data, ~ aov(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
#     aov_tidy  = map(aov_model, ~ broom::tidy(.x)),
#     resid     = map(aov_model, residuals),
#     shapiro   = map(resid, ~ shapiro.test(.x)),
#     bartlett  = map(data, ~ bartlett.test(values ~ FN, data = .x))
#   )
# 
# res_aov$shapiro

res_kw <- df_EOL_cp %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    kw_test  = map(data, ~ kruskal.test(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    kw_tidy  = map(kw_test, ~ broom::tidy(.x))
  )
res_kw$kw_tidy

# 2) Keep only compounds with p.value < 0.05
res_kw_sig <- res_kw %>% 
  mutate(p_value = map_dbl(kw_tidy, ~ .x$p.value)) %>% 
  filter(p_value < 0.05)

# 3) Dunn test for those significant compounds
res_dunn <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    dunn_raw = map(data, ~ FSA::dunnTest(values ~ FN, data = .x, method = "holm")),
    dunn_tbl = map(dunn_raw, ~ .x$res %>% as_tibble())
  )

# 4) Flat table of all post-hoc comparisons
dunn_table <- res_dunn %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, dunn_tbl) %>%   # use the real column name
  unnest(dunn_tbl)
dunn_table

# 3bis) Dunn test + letters for significant compounds only
res_letters <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    # Dunn all-pairs test per compound
    dunn_kw   = map(data, ~ kwAllPairsDunnTest(values ~ as.factor(FN),
                                               data = .x,
                                               p.adjust.method = "holm")),
    # matrix of adjusted p-values
    p_mat     = map(dunn_kw, ~ round(.x$p.value, digits = 3)),
    # full symmetric matrix required by multcompLetters
    p_full    = map(p_mat, fullPTable),
    # compact letter display
    letters   = map(p_full, ~ multcompLetters(.x)$Letters),
    # convert named vector of letters to tibble
    letters_df = map(letters, ~ tibble(
      FN = names(.x),
      label = as.vector(unlist(.x))
    ))
  )

letters_table_EOL_cp <- res_letters %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, letters_df) %>% 
  unnest(letters_df) %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(label) > 1) %>%   # keep compounds having ≥2 different labels
  ungroup()
  
letters_table_EOL_cp
```

```{r df with all letters KW including blanks}
results_KW_blc_cp <- bind_rows(
  letters_table_new_cp %>% mutate(state = "new"),
  letters_table_EOL_cp %>% mutate(state = "EOL")
) %>%
  #mutate(state = if_else(FN == "blank", "blank", state)) %>% 
  arrange(compounds, state, FN) %>% 
  #if state = new label in capital letters
  mutate(
    label = case_when(
      state == "new" ~ toupper(label),
      TRUE ~ label))
results_KW_blc_cp 
```

```{r Anova ou kruskal excluding blanks - new FNs}
# ANOVA or Kruskal-Wallis test including blanks
df_new_cp <- metal_orga_cp %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>%
  filter(state != "EOL", FN != "blank") 
df_new_cp

# res_aov <- df_new_cp %>% 
#   group_by(compounds) %>% 
#   nest() %>% 
#   mutate(
#     aov_model = map(data, ~ aov(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
#     aov_tidy  = map(aov_model, ~ broom::tidy(.x)),
#     resid     = map(aov_model, residuals),
#     shapiro   = map(resid, ~ shapiro.test(.x)),
#     bartlett  = map(data, ~ bartlett.test(values ~ FN, data = .x))
#   )
# 
# res_aov$shapiro

res_kw <- df_new_cp %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    kw_test  = map(data, ~ kruskal.test(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    kw_tidy  = map(kw_test, ~ broom::tidy(.x))
  )
res_kw$kw_tidy

# 2) Keep only compounds with p.value < 0.05
res_kw_sig <- res_kw %>% 
  mutate(p_value = map_dbl(kw_tidy, ~ .x$p.value)) %>% 
  filter(p_value < 0.05)

# 3) Dunn test for those significant compounds
res_dunn <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    dunn_raw = map(data, ~ FSA::dunnTest(values ~ FN, data = .x, method = "holm")),
    dunn_tbl = map(dunn_raw, ~ .x$res %>% as_tibble())
  )

# 4) Flat table of all post-hoc comparisons
dunn_table <- res_dunn %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, dunn_tbl) %>%   # use the real column name
  unnest(dunn_tbl)
dunn_table

# 3bis) Dunn test + letters for significant compounds only
res_letters <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    # Dunn all-pairs test per compound
    dunn_kw   = map(data, ~ kwAllPairsDunnTest(values ~ as.factor(FN),
                                               data = .x,
                                               p.adjust.method = "holm")),
    # matrix of adjusted p-values
    p_mat     = map(dunn_kw, ~ round(.x$p.value, digits = 3)),
    # full symmetric matrix required by multcompLetters
    p_full    = map(p_mat, fullPTable),
    # compact letter display
    letters   = map(p_full, ~ multcompLetters(.x)$Letters),
    # convert named vector of letters to tibble
    letters_df = map(letters, ~ tibble(
      FN = names(.x),
      label = as.vector(unlist(.x))
    ))
  )

letters_table_new_cp <- res_letters %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, letters_df) %>% 
  unnest(letters_df) %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(label) > 1) %>%   # keep compounds having ≥2 different labels
  ungroup()
  
letters_table_new_cp
```

```{r Anova ou kruskal including blanks - EOL FNs}
# ANOVA or Kruskal-Wallis test including blanks
df_EOL_cp <- metal_orga_cp %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>%
  filter(state != "new", FN != "blank") 
df_EOL_cp

# res_aov <- df_EOL_cp %>% 
#   group_by(compounds) %>% 
#   nest() %>% 
#   mutate(
#     aov_model = map(data, ~ aov(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
#     aov_tidy  = map(aov_model, ~ broom::tidy(.x)),
#     resid     = map(aov_model, residuals),
#     shapiro   = map(resid, ~ shapiro.test(.x)),
#     bartlett  = map(data, ~ bartlett.test(values ~ FN, data = .x))
#   )
# 
# res_aov$shapiro

res_kw <- df_EOL_cp %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  nest() %>% 
  mutate(
    kw_test  = map(data, ~ kruskal.test(values ~ FN, data = .x)),
    kw_tidy  = map(kw_test, ~ broom::tidy(.x))
  )
res_kw$kw_tidy

# 2) Keep only compounds with p.value < 0.05
res_kw_sig <- res_kw %>% 
  mutate(p_value = map_dbl(kw_tidy, ~ .x$p.value)) %>% 
  filter(p_value < 0.05)

# 3) Dunn test for those significant compounds
res_dunn <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    dunn_raw = map(data, ~ FSA::dunnTest(values ~ FN, data = .x, method = "holm")),
    dunn_tbl = map(dunn_raw, ~ .x$res %>% as_tibble())
  )

# 4) Flat table of all post-hoc comparisons
dunn_table <- res_dunn %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, dunn_tbl) %>%   # use the real column name
  unnest(dunn_tbl)
dunn_table

# 3bis) Dunn test + letters for significant compounds only
res_letters <- res_kw_sig %>% 
  mutate(
    # Dunn all-pairs test per compound
    dunn_kw   = map(data, ~ kwAllPairsDunnTest(values ~ as.factor(FN),
                                               data = .x,
                                               p.adjust.method = "holm")),
    # matrix of adjusted p-values
    p_mat     = map(dunn_kw, ~ round(.x$p.value, digits = 3)),
    # full symmetric matrix required by multcompLetters
    p_full    = map(p_mat, fullPTable),
    # compact letter display
    letters   = map(p_full, ~ multcompLetters(.x)$Letters),
    # convert named vector of letters to tibble
    letters_df = map(letters, ~ tibble(
      FN = names(.x),
      label = as.vector(unlist(.x))
    ))
  )

letters_table_EOL_cp <- res_letters %>% 
  dplyr::select(compounds, letters_df) %>% 
  unnest(letters_df) %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(label) > 1) %>%   # keep compounds having ≥2 different labels
  ungroup()
  
letters_table_EOL_cp
```

```{r df with all letters KW excluding blanks}
results_KW_nblc_cp <- bind_rows(
  letters_table_new_cp %>% mutate(state = "new"),
  letters_table_EOL_cp %>% mutate(state = "EOL")
) %>%
  #mutate(state = if_else(FN == "blank", "blank", state)) %>% 
  arrange(compounds, state, FN) %>% 
  #if state = new label in capital letters
  mutate(
    label = case_when(
      state == "new" ~ toupper(label),
      TRUE ~ label))
results_KW_nblc_cp 
```


```{r Statistics blank vs. FN and state}

#1) Prepare data

df <- metal_orga_cp %>%
  filter(!is.na(values))

# blank replicated for each state so comparisons are "compound + state"
blank_states <- tibble(state = c("new", "EOL"))

df_blank <- df %>%
  filter(FN == "blank") %>%
  dplyr::select(FN, compounds, values) %>%
  crossing(blank_states)

df_test <- df %>%
  filter(FN != "blank", state %in% c("new", "EOL")) %>%
  dplyr::select(FN, state, compounds, values)

# 2) Run MWU test: FN vs blank
#    for each (FN, state, compounds)

results_vs_blank <- df_test %>%
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>%
  summarise(
    n_FN = sum(!is.na(values)),
    x = list(values),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    df_blank %>%
      group_by(state, compounds) %>%
      summarise(
        n_blank = sum(!is.na(values)),
        y = list(values),
        .groups = "drop"
      ),
    by = c("state", "compounds")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # MWU only if both groups have enough data + at least some variability
    p_value = pmap_dbl(
      list(x, y, n_FN, n_blank),
      ~{
        if (..3 < 2 || ..4 < 2) return(NA_real_)
        if (dplyr::n_distinct(..1) < 2 && dplyr::n_distinct(..2) < 2) return(NA_real_)
        suppressWarnings(wilcox.test(..1, ..2, paired = FALSE)$p.value)
      }
    ),
    stars = case_when(
      is.na(p_value)  ~ NA_character_,
      p_value < 0.001 ~ "###",
      p_value < 0.01  ~ "##",
      p_value < 0.05  ~ "#",
      TRUE            ~ "ns"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(FN, state, compounds, n_FN, n_blank, p_value, stars) %>%
  arrange(compounds, state, FN)

results_vs_blank

```



```{r Statistics new vs EOL}
df_new_EOL_cp <- metal_orga_cp %>% 
  filter(FN != "blank") %>% 
  mutate(
    state     = as.factor(state),
    FN        = as.factor(FN),
    compounds = as.factor(compounds)
  ) 
df_new_EOL_cp

#Eliminate the FN and state for which the values of the three replicates were = 0
df_counts <-
  df_new_EOL_cp %>%
  group_by(FN, compounds, state) %>%
  summarise(
    n_zero = sum(!is.na(values_corr) & values_corr == 0),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

metals <- c("Ag", "As", "Al", "Fe", "Cu", "Mn", "Zn")

compounds_ok <- df_counts %>%
  group_by(FN, compounds) %>%
  filter(
    all(
      n_zero < if_else(first(compounds) %in% metals, 3L, 3L)
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  distinct(FN, compounds)

compounds_ok

df_new_EOL_cp_ok <-
  df_new_EOL_cp %>%
  semi_join(compounds_ok, by = c("FN", "compounds"))
df_new_EOL_cp_ok

results_tests <-
  df_new_EOL_cp_ok %>%
  group_by(compounds, FN) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    # simple one-way model: values_corr ~ state
    mod <- lm(values_corr ~ state, data = .x)
    
    # Shapiro–Wilk on residuals (normality)
    shapiro_p <- shapiro.test(residuals(mod))$p.value
    
    # Levene’s test (homogeneity of variance across states)
    lev_p <- leveneTest(values_corr ~ state, data = .x)$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    
    tibble(
      shapiro_p = shapiro_p,
      levene_p  = lev_p,
      n         = n_distinct(.x$state)
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup()
results_tests

# One MWU test per FN × compounds, comparing new vs EOL
tt_results_cp <- df_new_EOL_cp_ok %>% 
  group_by(FN, compounds) %>% 
  summarise(
    tt = list(t.test(values_corr ~ state, var.equal = T)),  # Mann-Whitney
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    tt_tidy = map(tt, broom::tidy)
  ) %>% 
  unnest(tt_tidy) %>% 
  mutate(
    diff = if_else(p.value < 0.0001, "****", 
                   if_else(p.value < 0.001, "***",
                           if_else(p.value < 0.01, "**",
                                   if_else(p.value < 0.05, "*", "ns"))))
  ) 
tt_results_cp

```

#Visualizations
```{r First visualization}
G_comp_cat <- Comp_cat %>% ggplot(aes(x = rep, y = values, fill = state)) + 
  geom_col(position = position_dodge()) +
  facet_grid(compounds~FN, scales = "free")
G_comp_cat

# G_all <- data2 %>% ggplot(aes(x = rep, y = values, fill = state)) + 
#   geom_col(position = position_dodge()) +
#   facet_grid(compounds~FN, scales = "free")
# G_all

G_avg <- data_avg %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>% 
  mutate(state = factor(state, levels = c("blank", "new", "EOL"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = state, y = avg, fill = FN)) + 
  geom_col(position = position_dodge()) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = avg, ymax = avg + sd), 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
                width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  facet_wrap(~compounds, scales = "free")
G_avg

G_avg_cat <- data_avg_cat %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>% 
  mutate(state = factor(state, levels = c("blank", "new", "EOL"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = state, y = avg, fill = FN)) + 
  geom_col(position = position_dodge()) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = avg, ymax = avg + sd), 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
                width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  facet_wrap(~compounds, scales = "free")
G_avg_cat

G_avg_corr <- data_avg_corr %>% 
  mutate(compounds = case_when(
      compounds == "acenaphtene" ~ "Acenaphtene",
      compounds == "acenaphtylene" ~ "Acenaphtylene",
      compounds == "anthracene" ~ "Anthracene",
      compounds == "benzo(ghi)péryléne" ~ "Benzo(ghi)perylene",
      compounds == "benzo[b+k]fluoranthene" ~ "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene",
      compounds == "benzoaanthracene" ~ "Benzo[a]anthracene",
      compounds == "benzoepyrene" ~ "Benzo[e]pyrene",
      compounds == "benzothiophene" ~ "Benzothiophene",
      compounds == "biphenyl" ~ "Biphenyl",
      compounds == "chrysene" ~ "Chrysene",
      compounds == "dibenzoahanthracene" ~ "Dibenzo[a,h]anthracene",
      compounds == "fluoranthene" ~ "Fluoranthene",
      compounds == "fluorene" ~ "Fluorene",
      compounds == "indeno123cdpyrene" ~ "Indeno[1,2,3-cd]pyrene",
      compounds == "naphtalene" ~ "Naphtalene",
      compounds == "pyrene" ~ "Pyrene",
      compounds == "4-tOP" ~ "4-tert-Octylphenol",
      compounds == "Nonylphénols" ~ "Nonylphenols",
      TRUE ~ compounds
      )) %>% 
  mutate(compounds = factor(compounds, levels = c(
      "Acenaphtene", "Acenaphtylene", "Anthracene", "Benzo[e]pyrene", "Benzo(ghi)perylene", "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene", "Benzo[a]anthracene", "Benzothiophene", "Biphenyl", "Chrysene", "Dibenzo[a,h]anthracene", "Dibenzothiophene", "Fluoranthene", "Fluorene", "Indeno[1,2,3-cd]pyrene", "Phenanthrene", "Naphtalene", "Pyrene",
      "BDE 28", "BDE 47", "BDE 99", "BDE 100", "BDE 153", "BDE 154", "BDE 183",
      "ATBC", "BBP", "DBP", "DEHA", "DEHP", "DEP", "DMP",
      "4-tert-Octylphenol", "Nonylphenols"))) %>% 
  mutate(state = factor(state, levels = c("blank", "new", "EOL"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = state, y = avg_corr, fill = FN)) + 
  geom_col(position = position_dodge()) +
  #scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = avg_corr, ymax = avg_corr + sd_corr), 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
                width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_real_FN5) +
  facet_wrap(~compounds, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    # title = "Concentrations of organic compounds in fishing net extracts",
    #    subtitle = "Values corrected by average blank (n = 4) subtraction\n",
       x = "State of the FNs", 
       y = "Average concentration (µg/L)", 
       fill = "Fishing net\nextracts") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 16, face = 'bold', angle = 0, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 16, face = 'bold'),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1.2)),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.3, 'cm'),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16, face = 'bold', color = "black"),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.85, "cm"),
    legend.key.width = unit(1, "cm"),
    legend.title.align = 0,
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.margin = unit(c(1, 1, 1, 1), "cm"),
    legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.3, "cm")
  ) 
G_avg_corr

G_avg_corr_cat <- metal_orga %>% 
  mutate(compounds = case_when(
      compounds == "AKP" ~ "Alkylphenols",
      compounds == "AKP_NP" ~ "Alkylphenols without Nonylphenols",
      compounds == "HAP" ~ "PAHs",
      compounds == "PBDE" ~ "PBDEs",
      compounds == "Phtalates" ~ "Phthalates",
      compounds == "metals" ~ "Trace metals",
      TRUE ~ compounds
      )) %>% 
  mutate(compounds = factor(compounds, levels = c(
      "PAHs", "PBDEs", "Phthalates", "Alkylphenols", "Alkylphenols without Nonylphenols", "Trace metals"))) %>%
  mutate(state = factor(state, levels = c("blank", "new", "EOL"))) %>%
  filter(compounds != "Alkylphenols", FN != "blank") %>%
  mutate(compounds = if_else(compounds == "Alkylphenols without Nonylphenols", "Alkylphenols", compounds)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = state, y = values_corr, color = compounds, shape = rep, group = compounds)) + 
  scale_y_log10(
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 10, 100, 1000),          # Set your desired breaks
    labels = label_number(trim = TRUE)           # Remove scientific format
  ) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), 
    size = 4) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(19, 18, 17)) + 
  #scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "PAHs" = "#1ae4b6",              # Dark goldenrod
      "PBDEs" = "#4686fb",             # Steel blue
      "Phthalates" = "#faba39",        # Vivid red
      "Alkylphenols" = "#770000",      # Strong green
      "Trace metals" = "#B12090FF"      # Strong green
    )) +
  #scale_fill_manual(values = Color_real_FN5) +
  facet_wrap(~FN) +
  labs(#title = "Concentrations of organic compounds in fishing net extracts",
       #subtitle = "Values corrected by average blank (n = 4) subtraction\n",
       x = "State of the FNs", 
       y = "Average concentration (µg/L)", 
       fill = "Organic compounds") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = -4, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = 4, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 15, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 20, l = 0)),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1.5)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", linewidth = 0.1),
        legend.title= element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = 1, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 10, l = 0)),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm'),
        legend.spacing.y = unit(0.5, 'cm'),
        legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "cm"),
        legend.position="right",
        panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines"),
        plot.margin = unit(c(1, 1, 2, 1), "cm")) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(title = "Plastic chemical \ncategories", order = 1),
    shape = guide_legend(title = "Replicates", order = 2))
G_avg_corr_cat

G_avg_corr_cat2 <- metal_orga %>% 
  #avg and sd with reframe
  group_by(FN, state, compounds) %>%
  reframe(
    n = n(),
    avg_corr = mean(values_corr, na.rm = T),
    sd_corr = sd(values_corr, na.rm = T)) %>%
  mutate(avg_corr = if_else(avg_corr <1, avg_corr+1, avg_corr),
         sd_corr = if_else(sd_corr <1, sd_corr+1, sd_corr)) %>%
  mutate(avg_corr_log = log10(avg_corr),
         sd_corr_log = log10(sd_corr)) %>%
  mutate(compounds = case_when(
      compounds == "AKP" ~ "Alkylphenols",
      compounds == "AKP_NP" ~ "Alkylphenols without Nonylphenols",
      compounds == "HAP" ~ "PAHs",
      compounds == "PBDE" ~ "PBDEs",
      compounds == "Phtalates" ~ "Phthalates",
      compounds == "metals" ~ "Trace metals",
      TRUE ~ compounds
      )) %>% 
  mutate(compounds = factor(compounds, levels = c(
      "PAHs", "PBDEs", "Phthalates", "Alkylphenols", "Alkylphenols without Nonylphenols", "Trace metals"))) %>%
  mutate(state = factor(state, levels = c("blank", "new", "EOL"))) %>%
  filter(compounds != "Alkylphenols without Nonylphenols", FN != "blank") %>%
  #mutate(compounds = if_else(compounds == "Alkylphenols without Nonylphenols", "Alkylphenols", compounds)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = state, y = avg_corr, 
             fill = compounds 
             #shape = rep, 
             #group = compounds
             )) + 
  scale_y_log10(
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 10, 100, 1000),          # Set your desired breaks
    labels = label_number(trim = TRUE)           # Remove scientific format
  ) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), 
    width = 0.5) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = avg_corr, ymax = if_else(avg_corr > 1, avg_corr + sd_corr, avg_corr)), 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), 
                width = 0.2) + 
  #scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "PAHs" = "#1ae4b6",              # Dark goldenrod
      "PBDEs" = "#4686fb",             # Steel blue
      "Phthalates" = "#faba39",        # Vivid red
      "Alkylphenols" = "#770000",      # Strong green
      "Trace metals" = "#B12090FF"      # Strong green
    )) +
  #scale_fill_manual(values = Color_real_FN5) +
  facet_wrap(~FN) +
  labs(#title = "Concentrations of organic compounds in fishing net extracts",
       #subtitle = "Values corrected by average blank (n = 4) subtraction\n",
       x = "State of the FNs", 
       y = "Average concentration (µg/L)", 
       fill = "Organic compounds") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = -4, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = 4, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 15, face = 'bold'),
        title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 20, l = 0)),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1.5)),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey", color = "grey", linewidth = 0.1),
        legend.title= element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = 1, margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 10, l = 0)),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, 'cm'),
        legend.spacing.y = unit(0.5, 'cm'),
        legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "cm"),
        legend.position="right",
        panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines"),
        plot.margin = unit(c(1, 1, 2, 1), "cm")) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(title = "Plastic chemical \ncategories", order = 1),
    shape = guide_legend(title = "Replicates", order = 2))
G_avg_corr_cat2

```

```{r visualization by compounds orga}
df_prepa_raw <- data_avg_corr %>% 
  mutate(compounds = case_when(
      compounds == "acenaphtene" ~ "Acenaphtene",
      compounds == "acenaphtylene" ~ "Acenaphtylene",
      compounds == "anthracene" ~ "Anthracene",
      compounds == "benzo(ghi)péryléne" ~ "Benzo(ghi)perylene",
      compounds == "benzo[b+k]fluoranthene" ~ "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene",
      compounds == "benzoaanthracene" ~ "Benzo[a]anthracene",
      compounds == "benzoepyrene" ~ "Benzo[e]pyrene",
      compounds == "benzothiophene" ~ "Benzothiophene",
      compounds == "biphenyl" ~ "Biphenyl",
      compounds == "chrysene" ~ "Chrysene",
      compounds == "dibenzoahanthracene" ~ "Dibenzo[a,h]anthracene",
      compounds == "fluoranthene" ~ "Fluoranthene",
      compounds == "fluorene" ~ "Fluorene",
      compounds == "indeno123cdpyrene" ~ "Indeno[1,2,3-cd]pyrene",
      compounds == "naphtalene" ~ "Naphthalene",
      compounds == "pyrene" ~ "Pyrene",
      compounds == "4-tOP" ~ "4-tert-Octylphenol",
      compounds == "Nonylphénols" ~ "Nonylphenols",
      TRUE ~ compounds
      )) %>% 
  mutate(compounds = factor(compounds, levels = c(
      "Acenaphtene", "Acenaphtylene", "Anthracene", "Benzo[e]pyrene", "Benzo(ghi)perylene", "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene", "Benzo[a]anthracene", "Benzothiophene", "Biphenyl", "Chrysene", "Dibenzo[a,h]anthracene", "Dibenzothiophene", "Fluoranthene", "Fluorene", "Indeno[1,2,3-cd]pyrene", "Phenanthrene", "Naphthalene", "Pyrene",
      "BDE 28", "BDE 47", "BDE 99", "BDE 100", "BDE 153", "BDE 154", "BDE 183",
      "ATBC", "BBP", "DBP", "DEHA", "DEHP", "DEP", "DMP",
      "4-tert-Octylphenol", "Nonylphenols"))) %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>%
  mutate(state = factor(state, levels = c("blank", "new", "EOL")))  
df_prepa_raw

G_compounds <- df_prepa_raw %>% 
  filter(FN != "blank") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = state, y = avg_corr, fill = FN)) +
  geom_col(
    width = 0.8,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = avg_corr, ymax = avg_corr + sd_corr),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  facet_wrap(~compounds, scales = "free_y") +
  ## add some headroom above the tallest bar/errorbar
  #scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +
  labs(
    x = "State of the FNs",
    y = "Average concentration (ng/L)",
    fill = "Fishing net\nextracts"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = -4,
                                margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = 4,
                                margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    strip.text   = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1.3)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", vjust = 1,
                                margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 10, l = 0)),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.margin = unit(c(1, 1, 2, 1), "cm")
  ) 
G_compounds

#graph without blank correction
df_prepa_raw <- data_avg_corr %>% 
  mutate(compounds = case_when(
      compounds == "acenaphtene" ~ "Acenaphtene",
      compounds == "acenaphtylene" ~ "Acenaphtylene",
      compounds == "anthracene" ~ "Anthracene",
      compounds == "benzo(ghi)péryléne" ~ "Benzo(ghi)perylene",
      compounds == "benzo[b+k]fluoranthene" ~ "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene",
      compounds == "benzoaanthracene" ~ "Benzo[a]anthracene",
      compounds == "benzoepyrene" ~ "Benzo[e]pyrene",
      compounds == "benzothiophene" ~ "Benzothiophene",
      compounds == "biphenyl" ~ "Biphenyl",
      compounds == "chrysene" ~ "Chrysene",
      compounds == "dibenzoahanthracene" ~ "Dibenzo[a,h]anthracene",
      compounds == "fluoranthene" ~ "Fluoranthene",
      compounds == "fluorene" ~ "Fluorene",
      compounds == "indeno123cdpyrene" ~ "Indeno[1,2,3-cd]pyrene",
      compounds == "naphtalene" ~ "Naphthalene",
      compounds == "pyrene" ~ "Pyrene",
      compounds == "4-tOP" ~ "4-tert-Octylphenol",
      compounds == "Nonylphénols" ~ "Nonylphenols",
      TRUE ~ compounds
      )) %>% 
  mutate(compounds = factor(compounds, levels = c(
      "Acenaphtene", "Acenaphtylene", "Anthracene", "Benzo[e]pyrene", "Benzo(ghi)perylene", "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene", "Benzo[a]anthracene", "Benzothiophene", "Biphenyl", "Chrysene", "Dibenzo[a,h]anthracene", "Dibenzothiophene", "Fluoranthene", "Fluorene", "Indeno[1,2,3-cd]pyrene", "Phenanthrene", "Naphthalene", "Pyrene",
      "BDE 28", "BDE 47", "BDE 99", "BDE 100", "BDE 153", "BDE 154", "BDE 183",
      "ATBC", "BBP", "DBP", "DEHA", "DEHP", "DEP", "DMP",
      "4-tert-Octylphenol", "Nonylphenols"))) %>% 
  mutate(state = if_else(is.na(state), "blank", state)) %>%
  mutate(state = factor(state, levels = c("blank", "new", "EOL"))) 
df_prepa_raw

tt_results_Gcp <- tt_results_cp %>%  dplyr::select(FN, compounds, diff) %>% 
  # duplicate each row exactly 2 times
  slice(rep(row_number(), each = 2)) %>% 
  # create the new variable with values "new" and "EOL"
  mutate(state = rep(c("new", "EOL"), times = nrow(tt_results_cp))) %>% 
  #mutate(diff = if_else(state == "new", "ns", diff)) %>% 
  arrange(compounds) %>% 
  group_by(compounds) %>% 
  filter(any(diff != "ns")) %>%  # keep compounds with at least one non-"ns"
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(compounds = case_when(
      compounds == "acenaphtene" ~ "Acenaphtene",
      compounds == "acenaphtylene" ~ "Acenaphtylene",
      compounds == "anthracene" ~ "Anthracene",
      compounds == "benzo(ghi)péryléne" ~ "Benzo(ghi)perylene",
      compounds == "benzo[b+k]fluoranthene" ~ "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene",
      compounds == "benzoaanthracene" ~ "Benzo[a]anthracene",
      compounds == "benzoepyrene" ~ "Benzo[e]pyrene",
      compounds == "benzothiophene" ~ "Benzothiophene",
      compounds == "biphenyl" ~ "Biphenyl",
      compounds == "chrysene" ~ "Chrysene",
      compounds == "dibenzoahanthracene" ~ "Dibenzo[a,h]anthracene",
      compounds == "fluoranthene" ~ "Fluoranthene",
      compounds == "fluorene" ~ "Fluorene",
      compounds == "indeno123cdpyrene" ~ "Indeno[1,2,3-cd]pyrene",
      compounds == "naphtalene" ~ "Naphthalene",
      compounds == "pyrene" ~ "Pyrene",
      compounds == "4-tOP" ~ "4-tert-Octylphenol",
      compounds == "Nonylphénols" ~ "Nonylphenols",
      TRUE ~ compounds
      )) %>% 
  mutate(state = factor(state, levels = c("new", "EOL")))
tt_results_Gcp

# match names and states between tables
results_KW_lab <- results_KW_blc_cp  %>% 
  mutate(compounds = case_when(
      compounds == "acenaphtene" ~ "Acenaphtene",
      compounds == "acenaphtylene" ~ "Acenaphtylene",
      compounds == "anthracene" ~ "Anthracene",
      compounds == "benzo(ghi)péryléne" ~ "Benzo(ghi)perylene",
      compounds == "benzo[b+k]fluoranthene" ~ "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene",
      compounds == "benzoaanthracene" ~ "Benzo[a]anthracene",
      compounds == "benzoepyrene" ~ "Benzo[e]pyrene",
      compounds == "benzothiophene" ~ "Benzothiophene",
      compounds == "biphenyl" ~ "Biphenyl",
      compounds == "chrysene" ~ "Chrysene",
      compounds == "dibenzoahanthracene" ~ "Dibenzo[a,h]anthracene",
      compounds == "fluoranthene" ~ "Fluoranthene",
      compounds == "fluorene" ~ "Fluorene",
      compounds == "indeno123cdpyrene" ~ "Indeno[1,2,3-cd]pyrene",
      compounds == "naphtalene" ~ "Naphthalene",
      compounds == "pyrene" ~ "Pyrene",
      compounds == "4-tOP" ~ "4-tert-Octylphenol",
      compounds == "Nonylphénols" ~ "Nonylphenols",
      TRUE ~ compounds
      )) %>% 
  mutate(y_top = case_when(
      compounds == "Anthracene" ~ 3.2,
      compounds == "Benzo(ghi)perylene" ~ 6.2,
      compounds == "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene" ~ 4.2,
      compounds == "Benzo[a]anthracene" ~ 7.5,
      compounds == "Biphenyl" ~ 17,
      compounds == "Chrysene" ~ 25,
      compounds == "Fluoranthene" ~ 35,
      compounds == "Phenanthrene" ~ 60,
      compounds == "Naphthalene" ~ 38,
      compounds == "Pyrene" ~ 36,
      compounds == "ATBC" ~ 42,
      compounds == "DEHA" ~ 190,
      compounds == "4-tert-Octylphenol" ~ 17,
      compounds == "Nonylphenols" ~ 5300,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>% 
  #change order state
  mutate(state = factor(state, levels = c("blank", "new", "EOL"))) %>% 
  mutate(label = if_else(compounds == "Phenanthrene" & state == "EOL", NA, label)) %>% 
  na.omit()

results_KW_lab

df_cp_stat <- df_prepa_raw %>% 
  filter(compounds %in% c("Anthracene", "Benzo(ghi)perylene", "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene","Benzo[a]anthracene", "Biphenyl",
                          "Chrysene", "Fluoranthene", "Phenanthrene", "Naphthalene", "Pyrene",
                          "ATBC", "DEHA", 
                          "Nonylphenols", "4-tert-Octylphenol"))
df_cp_stat 

pos_dodge <- position_dodge(width = 0.5)

Gcp_no_corr <- df_cp_stat %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = FN, y = avg, fill = FN, pattern = state, group = state)) +
  geom_bar_pattern(
    stat = "identity",
    width = 0.5,
    position = pos_dodge,
    pattern_density = 0.15,
    pattern_fill = "white",
    pattern_color = "white",
    pattern_spacing = 0.04,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = avg, ymax = avg + sd),
    position = pos_dodge,
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_pattern_discrete(
    na.value = "none",
    choices = c("new" = "none", "EOL" = "stripe", "blank" = "circle"),
    limits = c("new", "EOL", "blank")
  ) +
  #scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = Color_real_FN5) +
  facet_wrap(~compounds, scales = "free_y") +
  ## add some headroom above the tallest bar/errorbar
  #scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +
  labs(
    x = "Fishing nets",
    y = "Average concentration (µg/L)",
    fill = "Fishing net",
    pattern = "State"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"#, vjust = -4,
                                #margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)
                                ),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"#, vjust = -4,
                                #margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)
                                ),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    strip.text   = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1.5)),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"#, vjust = -4,
                                #margin = margin(t = 0, r = 5, b = 0, l = 0)
                                ),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    legend.position = "right"#,
    #aspect.ratio = 1/2,
    #plot.margin = grid::unit(c(0.3, 0, 0.7, 0.7), "cm")
  ) +
  rotate_x_text(40)

Gcp_no_corr +
  geom_text(
    data = results_KW_lab,
    aes(x = FN, y = y_top, label = label, group = state, hjust = if_else(state == "new", 0.7, 
                    if_else(state == "EOL", -0.3, 0))),
    position = pos_dodge,
    size = 4
    ) +
  ggpubr::geom_bracket(
    data = tibble(
      compounds  = "4-tert-Octylphenol",  # adapt to your facet name if needed
      xmin       = c(4.88, 6.88, 7.88),
      xmax       = c(5.12, 7.12, 8.12),
      y.position = c(6, 9.5, 14),
      label      = c("**", "***", "**")
    ),
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, y.position = y.position, label = label),
    size       = 1.06,
    label.size = 4,
    vjust      = 0.3,
    tip.length = c(0.03, 0.03),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  ggpubr::geom_bracket(
    data = tibble(
      compounds  = "Anthracene",  # adapt to your facet name if needed
      xmin       = c(2.88, 6.88),
      xmax       = c(3.12, 7.12),
      y.position = c(0.7, 2.8),
      label      = c("*", "****")
    ),
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, y.position = y.position, label = label),
    size       = 1.06,
    label.size = 4,
    vjust      = 0.3,
    tip.length = c(0.03, 0.03),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  ggpubr::geom_bracket(
    data = tibble(
      compounds  = "ATBC",  # adapt to your facet name if needed
      xmin       = c(4.88, 6.88),
      xmax       = c(5.12, 7.12),
      y.position = c(43, 39),
      label      = c("**", "**")
    ),
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, y.position = y.position, label = label),
    size       = 1.06,
    label.size = 4,
    vjust      = 0.3,
    tip.length = c(0.03, 0.03),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  ggpubr::geom_bracket(
    data = tibble(
      compounds  = "Benzo[a]anthracene",  # adapt to your facet name if needed
      xmin       = c(2.88, 6.88),
      xmax       = c(3.12, 7.12),
      y.position = c(1.5, 2.3),
      label      = c("*", "***")
    ),
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, y.position = y.position, label = label),
    size       = 1.06,
    label.size = 4,
    vjust      = 0.3,
    tip.length = c(0.03, 0.03),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  ggpubr::geom_bracket(
    data = tibble(
      compounds  = "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene",  # adapt to your facet name if needed
      xmin       = c(6.88, 8.88),
      xmax       = c(7.12, 9.12),
      y.position = c(3.6, 2),
      label      = c("**", "**")
    ),
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, y.position = y.position, label = label),
    size       = 1.06,
    label.size = 4,
    vjust      = 0.3,
    tip.length = c(0.03, 0.03),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  ggpubr::geom_bracket(
    data = tibble(
      compounds  = "Biphenyl",  # adapt to your facet name if needed
      xmin       = c(5.88),
      xmax       = c(6.12),
      y.position = c(3.5),
      label      = c("**")
    ),
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, y.position = y.position, label = label),
    size       = 1.06,
    label.size = 4,
    vjust      = 0.3,
    tip.length = c(0.03, 0.03),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  ggpubr::geom_bracket(
    data = tibble(
      compounds  = "Chrysene",  # adapt to your facet name if needed
      xmin       = c(6.88),
      xmax       = c(7.12),
      y.position = c(9),
      label      = c("*")
    ),
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, y.position = y.position, label = label),
    size       = 1.06,
    label.size = 4,
    vjust      = 0.3,
    tip.length = c(0.03, 0.03),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  ggpubr::geom_bracket(
    data = tibble(
      compounds  = "DEHA",  # adapt to your facet name if needed
      xmin       = c(6.88),
      xmax       = c(7.12),
      y.position = c(163),
      label      = c("*")
    ),
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, y.position = y.position, label = label),
    size       = 1.06,
    label.size = 4,
    vjust      = 0.3,
    tip.length = c(0.03, 0.03),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  ggpubr::geom_bracket(
    data = tibble(
      compounds  = "Naphthalene",  # adapt to your facet name if needed
      xmin       = c(6.88, 7.88),
      xmax       = c(7.12, 8.12),
      y.position = c(41, 27),
      label      = c("****", "**")
    ),
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, y.position = y.position, label = label),
    size       = 1.06,
    label.size = 4,
    vjust      = 0.3,
    tip.length = c(0.03, 0.03),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  ggpubr::geom_bracket(
    data = tibble(
      compounds  = "Phenanthrene",  # adapt to your facet name if needed
      xmin       = c(3.88, 6.88),
      xmax       = c(4.12, 7.12),
      y.position = c(13, 50),
      label      = c("**", "**")
    ),
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, y.position = y.position, label = label),
    size       = 1.06,
    label.size = 4,
    vjust      = 0.3,
    tip.length = c(0.03, 0.03),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) 

#-------------------Plot with blank correction-------------

results_KW_lab <- results_KW_nblc_cp  %>% 
  mutate(compounds = case_when(
      compounds == "acenaphtene" ~ "Acenaphtene",
      compounds == "acenaphtylene" ~ "Acenaphtylene",
      compounds == "anthracene" ~ "Anthracene",
      compounds == "benzo(ghi)péryléne" ~ "Benzo(ghi)perylene",
      compounds == "benzo[b+k]fluoranthene" ~ "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene",
      compounds == "benzoaanthracene" ~ "Benzo[a]anthracene",
      compounds == "benzoepyrene" ~ "Benzo[e]pyrene",
      compounds == "benzothiophene" ~ "Benzothiophene",
      compounds == "biphenyl" ~ "Biphenyl",
      compounds == "chrysene" ~ "Chrysene",
      compounds == "dibenzoahanthracene" ~ "Dibenzo[a,h]anthracene",
      compounds == "fluoranthene" ~ "Fluoranthene",
      compounds == "fluorene" ~ "Fluorene",
      compounds == "indeno123cdpyrene" ~ "Indeno[1,2,3-cd]pyrene",
      compounds == "naphtalene" ~ "Naphthalene",
      compounds == "pyrene" ~ "Pyrene",
      compounds == "4-tOP" ~ "4-tert-Octylphenol",
      compounds == "Nonylphénols" ~ "Nonylphenols",
      TRUE ~ compounds
      )) %>% 
  mutate(y_top = case_when(
      compounds == "Anthracene" ~ 3.2,
      compounds == "Benzo(ghi)perylene" ~ 5.2,
      compounds == "Benzo[b+k]fluoranthene" ~ 3.8,
      compounds == "Benzo[a]anthracene" ~ 7.5,
      compounds == "Biphenyl" ~ 17,
      compounds == "Chrysene" ~ 25,
      compounds == "Fluoranthene" ~ 35,
      compounds == "Phenanthrene" ~ 60,
      compounds == "Naphthalene" ~ 70,
      compounds == "Pyrene" ~ 36,
      compounds == "ATBC" ~ 42,
      compounds == "DEHA" ~ 190,
      compounds == "4-tert-Octylphenol" ~ 16,
      compounds == "Nonylphenols" ~ 4900,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )) %>% 
  #change order state
  mutate(state = factor(state, levels = c("blank", "new", "EOL"))) %>% 
  # mutate(label = if_else(compounds == "Phenanthrene" & state == "EOL", NA, label)) %>% 
  na.omit()

results_KW_lab

Gcp_no_corr <- df_cp_stat %>% 
  filter(FN != "blank") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = FN, y = avg_corr, fill = FN, pattern = state, group = state)) +
  geom_bar_pattern(
    stat = "identity",
    width = 0.5,
    position = pos_dodge,
    pattern_density = 0.15,
    pattern_fill = "white",
    pattern_color = "white",
    pattern_spacing = 0.04,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = avg_corr, ymax = avg_corr + sd_corr),
    position = pos_dodge,
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_pattern_discrete(
    na.value = "none",
    choices = c("new" = "none", "EOL" = "stripe"),
```
